title: Matryoshka: Recursive and Coherent Generation of Plausible City-Scale 3D Environments
teaser: Generate plausible city-scale 3D environments through a recursive pipeline that decomposes the world from city layout down to individual room interiors. The focus is structural plausibility, not photorealism.
abstract: Most prior work on 3D environment generation targets either outdoor scenes (city blocks, facades) or indoor scenes (rooms, furniture), but rarely both within a coherent pipeline and mainly focus on image quality. This project aims to bridge that gap with a hierarchical, recursive generator. A coarse layout for a city chunk is produced first, either via image-space or scene-layout diffusion in the spirit of [DiffuScene] and [CityDreamer], or autoregressively in the spirit of [ATISS], using OpenStreetMap chunks as training data. The generated layout is then expanded into three specialized branches that refine houses, streets, and nature respectively. House structure — number of floors, placement of doors, windows, and staircases — is generated coarsely by an LLM and translated into a mesh, in the spirit of [AnyHome]. Rooms inside houses are populated by another LLM-driven module that places objects retrieved from a large 3D asset database, similar to [Holodeck]. Each recursion step should be provide context from previous steps, e.g. doors open to the street, a balkony is facing the calmer side, bathrooms within one building are stacked on top of each other, etc. An LLM/VLM critique loop refines outputs at each stage, mirroring the iterative refinement strategies used in [SceneCraft] and [UrbanWorld]. The student will explore representation choices at each scale, design the interfaces between scales, and build a critique loop that catches structural failures (overlapping buildings, doors leading to walls, unreachable rooms) early. The exact focus of the project can change, based on preferences and opportunity costs..
milestones: Prototype the city-layout module on OpenStreetMap chunks (diffusion or autoregressive); iterate until generated layouts are visually plausible.

Build the LLM-to-mesh pipeline for houses (floors, doors, windows, staircases) and an LLM-driven room layout module with object retrieval; rendering should be done with blender; iterate with a VLM critique loop on a handful of building and room types.
Define and run a thorough evaluation — structural validity checks, FID on rasterized layouts, and e.g. qualitative comparisons against [UrbanWorld], [Holodeck], and [CityCraft] baselines.
Scale to full city chunks including the street and nature branches and re-evaluate the full pipeline end-to-end.
